title: That bright light after sunset? Venus is starting a summer show that's impossible to miss
summary: Venus is currently the brightest object in the evening sky after sunset, making it highly visible and a notable sight for stargazers throughout May and beyond.

title: NASA reveals new details about Artemis 3 astronaut mission
summary: NASA disclosed new details about the Artemis 3 astronaut mission to Earth orbit, planned for a late 2027 launch. This marks a key step in the agency's lunar exploration program.

title: Scientists propose new way to find aliens — and we may already have a spacecraft that can help
summary: Scientists propose a new method to detect alien life by analyzing the distribution of amino acids, suggesting existing spacecraft could aid in this search. This approach aims to distinguish between biologically produced amino acids and those from non-living chemical reactions.

title: Remember 'Choose Your Own Adventure' books? Now there's a gorgeous graphic novel version heading to 'Space and Beyond' (exclusive)
summary: A graphic novel adaptation of the 1980s 'Choose Your Own Adventure' book 'Space and Beyond' is being released as an interactive comic. The project aims to bring the nostalgic space-themed adventure to a new generation.

title: Will Starship launch from foreign shores? SpaceX 'constantly exploring' options for megarocket liftoff sites
summary: SpaceX is actively exploring additional launch sites for its Starship megarocket, considering locations both within and outside the United States. The company aims to expand its launch capabilities beyond existing facilities.

title: What flings mysteriously powerful particles called 'cosmic rays' at Earth?
summary: High-energy cosmic rays, 10 million times more powerful than Earth's strongest particle accelerator, may hold a superheavy secret that could solve a 60-year-old mystery. These particles, called 'cosmic rays,' continue to puzzle scientists with their origin and nature.

title: Astrophotographer captures remarkable view of galactic 'Eyes' with backyard telescope
summary: An astrophotographer captured a remarkable image of a string of galaxies resembling 'Eyes' using a backyard telescope. The galaxies are part of the massive Virgo Galaxy Cluster.

title: Blue Origin's lunar lander mockup is ready for NASA Artemis astronaut training
summary: Blue Origin has completed assembly of a mockup of its Blue Moon Mark 2 lunar lander at NASA's Johnson Space Center for Artemis astronaut training. The mockup is now ready for astronauts to begin familiarization and training sessions.

title: Lasers shine a new light on the space junk air pollution problem
summary: Scientists have developed a new laser-based technique to analyze space debris and its impact on Earth's atmosphere. This method aims to better understand and mitigate the growing issue of space junk pollution.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to ISS scrubbed due to weather
summary: SpaceX's CRS-34 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS) was scrubbed due to weather conditions. The mission was scheduled shortly after the Progress MS-34 docked to the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are set to conduct nine launches in the week of May 11, marking a busy period for space activities. The launches span multiple locations in the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event was reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station, while its commercial sector is advancing towards reusability in space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion after Flight 11, with Flight 12 approaching. The upgrade work is part of ongoing preparations for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two SpaceX Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before a busier launch schedule.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming mission aims to build on previous test flights with improved performance and safety measures.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute deployment test was conducted, likely to ensure proper functionality in extreme conditions. The test was referred to as a 'bake-out' to simulate high temperatures.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company, along with mentorship and support for defense market entry and fundraising.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined preliminary plans for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch to test critical capabilities for a subsequent Moon landing in 2028. The mission will involve astronauts traveling to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) as part of the preparation.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking its entry into the space launch industry. This achievement establishes LEAP as a new competitor in the commercial launch sector.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by Gen. Michael Guetlein in March.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to deploy an Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device on the ISS. The automated mini lab is designed to fit into standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Administrator Jared Isaacman took over. His new role has been described as turning political.

title: Star Catcher Closes $65M Series A
summary: Star Catcher has secured $65 million in a Series A funding round led by B Capital, with Shield Capital and Cerberus Ventures co-leading the investment.

title: Fenix Space Flies Tow-Launch Prototype
summary: Fenix Space has successfully flown a tow-launch prototype, marking a significant milestone in their development. This achievement brings them closer to their goal of launching reusable space vehicles.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands, Pivots Business—and Raises $275M
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded to Cowboy Space Corporation and raised $275 million to align with its new business direction.

title: SOCOM Taps SkyFi to Build Tactical EO Imagery Tools
summary: SOCOM has selected SkyFi to develop a prototype for a sovereign intelligence platform that will provide troops with faster and easier access to geospatial imagery. The platform aims to enhance tactical decision-making through improved EO (electro-optical) imagery tools.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch’s Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.

title: Neo Space Group Selects ThinKom Antenna for Narrow-Body Installations
summary: Neo Space Group has chosen ThinKom's ThinAir Ka2517 multi-orbit phased array antenna for narrow-body aircraft installations across multiple airlines. ThinKom announced the partnership, highlighting its expansion into the aviation sector.

title: Eutelsat Shows Strong Growth in LEO Services and Mobility in Q3
summary: Eutelsat reported strong growth in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services and mobility for Q3, with LEO-driven revenue increasing by 65% year-over-year. The company's overall revenue reached 293 million euros ($343 million).

title: Bringing LEO to Private Networks: Logos Space Engineering VP Explains the Constellation’s Enterprise-Focused Design
summary: Logos Space Engineering is developing a satellite constellation focused on private and secure networks for enterprises and governments. The company is advancing its plans after receiving FCC approval.

title: Networks Business Propels Revenue Growth for SES, Operator Cancels 2 GEO Sats
summary: SES's Networks business drives revenue growth, while the company cancels two geostationary (GEO) satellites. The operator's financial results align with analyst expectations.

title: AST SpaceMobile Confirms Target for 45 BlueBirds This Year, Despite Blue Origin Launch Failure
summary: AST SpaceMobile aims to deploy 45 BlueBird satellites in 2024, despite a recent Blue Origin launch failure. CEO Abel Avellan reaffirmed this target during an investor update.

title: Star Catcher Raises $65M Series A to Fuel In-Orbit Power Grid
summary: Star Catcher, led by former Made in Space and Redwire executive Andrew Rush, raised $65 million in Series A funding to develop an in-orbit power grid. The startup aims to build infrastructure to support space-based energy distribution.

title: TrustPoint Receives $4M SpaceWERX Tactical Funding for Independent PNT System
summary: TrustPoint secured a $4 million SpaceWERX Tactical Funding Increase (TACFI) contract to develop four satellites and four ground stations for an independent PNT system demonstration. The funding supports the buildout of a full end-to-end system.

title: Space42 Enhances D2D Prospects with Skylo Partnership
summary: Space42 partnered with Skylo Technologies to enhance direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity using its Thuraya-4 geostationary satellite. This collaboration aims to deliver standards-based D2D services.

title: Vitesse Systems and Accelint Become Part of New Defense Tech Company Lyntris
summary: Vitesse Systems and Accelint have merged to form Lyntris, a new defense technology company specializing in connected battlefield solutions. Both companies were previously owned by Trive Capital.
